description: $20B regional bank board cyber maturity report. Six Function radar with RECOVER (DR/BCP) as the lagging Function at T1. Full GOVERN narrative across all 6 GOVERN Categories. 12-month capital plan with 6 investment lines, each mapped to a regulatory anchor (OCC, NY DFS, FFIEC).

board_talking_points: Mission and regulatory obligations documented (OCC Heightened Standards §III.A)
Risk appetite approved by board Risk Committee, reviewed annually
CISO reports to CRO; quarterly to board; ad hoc on material incidents
Third-party risk program covers 412 critical vendors, including 18 fourth-party dependencies
Cybersecurity policy hierarchy: enterprise policy + 14 domain policies + 47 procedures
Independent assurance: external audit (Big 4) + internal audit (5 FTE) + OCC examination

capital_plan_12mo.investment_line: Identity & Access Management modernization (privileged access management rollout)
capital_plan_12mo.regulatory_rationale: OCC Heightened Standards §III.C.3 (third-party access to sensitive systems)

capital_plan_12mo.investment_line: Third-party risk management program (continuous monitoring of critical vendors)
capital_plan_12mo.regulatory_rationale: OCC Heightened Standards §III.D (third-party relationship risk); NY DFS §500.11 (third-party service provider security policy)

capital_plan_12mo.investment_line: Security operations center uplift (24/7 monitoring + threat intelligence)
capital_plan_12mo.regulatory_rationale: FFIEC CAT Domain 4 (Continuous Monitoring); FFIEC IT Examination Handbook

capital_plan_12mo.investment_line: Disaster recovery & resilience testing (4 recovery exercises, hot-site failover)
capital_plan_12mo.regulatory_rationale: FFIEC CAT Domain 5 (Incident Management); OCC Heightened Standards §III.E (operational resilience)

capital_plan_12mo.investment_line: Cybersecurity training & awareness (board training + phishing simulation + role-based training)
GRC tooling consolidation (replacing 3 point tools with 1 platform)
capital_plan_12mo.regulatory_rationale: OCC Heightened Standards §III.A (governance); reduces audit fatigue for FFIEC examinations
